title: Alaska sues GoFundMe, PayPal, others over thousands of unauthorized charity pages

paragraphs.english: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (KTUU) - Alaska Attorney General Stephen Cox announced Tuesday that the state has filed lawsuits against six crowdfunding and charity-related platforms, accusing them of creating online donation pages for nonprofits without the organizations’ knowledge or consent and then soliciting contributions through those pages.
paragraphs.pidgin: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (KTUU) - Alaska Attorney General Stephen Cox announce Tuesday dat di state don filed lawsuits against six crowdfunding and charity-related platforms, accusing dem of creating online donation pages for nonprofits without di organizations’ knowledge or consent and then soliciting contributions through dem ones pages.

paragraphs.english: The lawsuits name GoFundMe, PayPal Inc., Charity Navigator, JustGiving, Pledgeto and Network for Good. Cox said the platforms used publicly available information to generate fundraising pages for more than 1 million nonprofits nationwide, including several thousand in Alaska, without first obtaining permission from the charities.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di lawsuits name GoFundMe, PayPal Inc., Charity Navigator, JustGiving, Pledgeto and Network for Good. Cox tok di platforms use publicly available information to generate fundraising pages for more than 1 million nonprofits nationwide, including many thousand for Alaska, without first obtaining permission from di charities.

paragraphs.english: “Generosity depends on trust,” Cox said in a statement. He said some Alaskans may have donated believing they were supporting a specific charity even though the organization did not authorize the page and “may never have received the donation — or may have received less than donors intended because of fees.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “Generosity depends on trust,” Cox tok for a statement. Im tok some Alaskans may don donated believing dem bin dey supporte a specific charity even though di organization no authorize di page and “may never don receive di donation — or may don receive less than donors intended because of fees.”

paragraphs.english: The attorney general’s office said GoFundMe created 1.4 million functional charity pages in fall 2025 that allowed donations. Cox’s office said GoFundMe may have created unauthorized pages for as many as 5,000 Alaska-based charities, often without the nonprofits’ knowledge.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di attorney general’s office tok GoFundMe create 1.4 million functional charity pages for fall 2025 dat allowed donations. Cox’s office tok GoFundMe may don create unauthorized pages for as plenti as 5,000 Alaska-based charities, often without di nonprofits’ knowledge.

paragraphs.english: State officials said the investigation began after Alaska nonprofits started reporting suspicious fundraising pages appearing online without their involvement.
paragraphs.pidgin: State officials tok di investigation start after Alaska nonprofits start reporting suspicious fundraising pages appearing online without dem involvement.

paragraphs.english: “Anyone Alaskans can call us,” said Mark Cucci, a senior assistant attorney general in the state’s Consumer Protection Unit.
paragraphs.pidgin: “Anybody Alaskans fit call us,” tok Mark Cucci, a senior assistant attorney general for di state’s Consumer Protection Unit.

paragraphs.english: “We actually heard from some local charities about this.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “We actually hear from some local charities about dis.”

paragraphs.english: Cucci said concerns spread quickly through Alaska’s nonprofit community after reports surfaced late last year.
paragraphs.pidgin: Cucci tok concerns spread sharp sharp through Alaska’s nonprofit community after reports surfaced late last year.

paragraphs.english: “The nonprofits are a close-knit community here,” he said. “They kind of got together and got the word out that there were these unauthorized donation pages on the GoFundMe site for them.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “Di nonprofits dey a close-knit community here,” im tok. “Dem kind of get together and get di word out dat e dey dis ones unauthorized donation pages on di GoFundMe site for dem.”

paragraphs.english: Investigators later found similar pages on multiple platforms.
paragraphs.pidgin: Investigators later find similar pages on many platforms.

paragraphs.english: “They found that there were unauthorized pages on other sites with active donation buttons where donors could donate to the charity without the charity ever having any knowledge that these pages were created for them,” Cucci said.
paragraphs.pidgin: “Dem find tok e dey unauthorized pages on other sites wit active donation buttons where donors fit donate to di charity without di charity ever having any knowledge dat dis ones pages bin dey create for dem,” Cucci tok.

paragraphs.english: The state’s Consumer Protection Unit said its investigation found other platforms engaged in similar conduct, including hosting pages that appeared to represent Alaska nonprofits even when the organizations had not agreed to allow the platforms to solicit donations on their behalf.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di state’s Consumer Protection Unit tok im investigation find other platforms engaged for similar conduct, including hosting pages dat appeared to represent Alaska nonprofits even when di organizations bin get no agree to allow di platforms to solicit donations on dem behalf.

paragraphs.english: State attorneys argue that nonprofits have a right to control fundraising in their name, including the strategies they use, the vendors and platforms they partner with, and how they manage donor relationships. The state said unauthorized pages can collect fees, display outdated or inaccurate information, compete with a nonprofit’s own campaigns, or prevent the organization from knowing who donated and when.
paragraphs.pidgin: State attorneys argue dat nonprofits get a correct to control fundraising for dem name, including di strategies dem use, di vendors and platforms dem partner wit, and how dem manage donor relationships. Di state tok unauthorized pages fit collect fees, display outdated or inaccurate information, compete wit a nonprofit’s own campaigns, or prevent di organization from knowing who donated and when.

paragraphs.english: The lawsuits allege violations of Alaska’s Charitable Solicitations Act, which the state says requires fundraisers to obtain consent before raising money for a charity, as well as the Alaska Consumer Protection Act.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di lawsuits allege violations of Alaska’s Charitable Solicitations Act, which di state tok requires fundraisers to obtain consent before raising money for a charity, and also di Alaska Consumer Protection Act.

paragraphs.english: The Foraker Group, a nonprofit support organization based in Alaska, praised the legal action.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di Foraker Group, a nonprofit support organization based for Alaska, praised di legal action.

paragraphs.english: “Philanthropy relies on the ability to honor donor intent and donor trust,” said Laurie Wolf, the group’s president and CEO, adding that such transactions lack nonprofit consent, transparency and accountability.
paragraphs.pidgin: “Philanthropy relies on di ability to honor donor intent and donor trust,” tok Laurie Wolf, di group’s president and CEO, adding dat such transactions lack nonprofit consent, transparency and accountability.

paragraphs.english: Cucci said the Foraker Group helped alert state officials and connect investigators with affected charities.
paragraphs.pidgin: Cucci tok di Foraker Group help alert state officials and connect investigators wit affected charities.

paragraphs.english: “They were the ones that I believe contacted us initially,” Cucci said. “So we worked with them and we opened an investigation at that point.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “dem bin dey di ones dat I believe contacted us initially,” Cucci tok. “So we work wit dem and we open an investigation for dat point.”

paragraphs.english: According to investigators, several Alaska charities said they had no knowledge certain donation pages existed.
paragraphs.pidgin: as investigators, many Alaska charities tok dem bin get no knowledge certain donation pages existed.

paragraphs.english: The state is seeking court orders requiring the companies to remove unauthorized donation pages for Alaska charities and is seeking civil penalties for each alleged violation of Alaska’s Unfair Trade Practices and Charitable Solicitations laws.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di state dey seeke court orders requiring di companies to remove unauthorized donation pages for Alaska charities and dey seeke civil penalties for each alleged violation of Alaska’s Unfair Trade Practices and Charitable Solicitations laws.

paragraphs.english: Under Alaska’s Consumer Protection Act, civil penalties for each violation could range from $1,000 to $25,000, according to Cucci.
paragraphs.pidgin: Under Alaska’s Consumer Protection Act, civil penalties for each violation fit range from $1,000 to $25,000, as Cucci.

paragraphs.english: Cucci said the state’s top priority is forcing platforms to remove pages that were created without nonprofit consent.
paragraphs.pidgin: Cucci tok di state’s top priority dey force platforms to remove pages dat bin dey create without nonprofit consent.

paragraphs.english: Investigators also want to determine how much money has been collected through these pages, and how much ultimately reached the charities. Cucci noted that many donation platforms take fees or optional tips that reduce the amount charities receive.
paragraphs.pidgin: Investigators also want to determine how plenti money don dey collected through dis ones pages, and how plenti ultimately reached di charities. Cucci note tok plenti donation platforms take fees or optional tips dat reduce di amount charities receive.

paragraphs.english: In a statement, a spokesperson for GoFundMe said the company created nonprofit pages using publicly available information and the donations were directed to the intended organizations.
paragraphs.pidgin: For a statement, a spokesperson for GoFundMe tok di company create nonprofit pages using publicly available information and di donations bin dey directed to di intended organizations.

paragraphs.english: “GoFundMe’s mission is to help people help each other by making it easier for donors to discover and support the causes they care about. We are committed to helping nonprofits reach new supporters by connecting them with the millions of people on our platform who want to make a difference. Nonprofit Pages were created using publicly available information to help people support nonprofit organizations, with donations going to the intended nonprofit.”
paragraphs.pidgin: “GoFundMe’s mission na to help pipul help each other by making e easier for donors to discover and support di causes dem care about. we dey committed to helping nonprofits reach new supporters by connecting dem wit di millions of pipul on our platform who want to make a difference. Nonprofit Pages bin dey create using publicly available information to help pipul support nonprofit organizations, wit donations going to di intended nonprofit.”
